questions.question: What is the topic for Week 2?
options: B. THE DEITY OF JESUS PART 1
C. THE GODHEAD AND THE TRINITY
questions.explanation: The topic provided for Week 2 is THE DEITY OF JESUS PART 1.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 1?
options: A. THE DEITY OF JESUS PART 1
questions.explanation: Theos Basileia was the topic for Week 1.

questions.question: According to the introduction, what is the definition of 'polytheistic'?
options: A. Belief in one true God
D. Belief that God is the universe
questions.explanation: The introduction defines polytheistic religion as '(belief in many gods)'.

questions.question: What is the term used to describe a 'god or goddess' or a being of divine status, quality, or nature?
questions.explanation: The introduction defines a 'deity' as a god or goddess, or a being of divine status.

questions.question: People of other major religions commonly argue about the Deity of Jesus Christ, teaching that He was only a prophet, a good teacher, or a godly man.
questions.explanation: The introduction mentions this is a common argument among other major religions.

questions.question: And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us an ___________, that we may know Him who is true...
questions.explanation: The memory verse (1 John 5:20) says 'has given us an understanding'.

questions.question: ...and we are in Him who is true, in His Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God and ________ life.
questions.explanation: The verse concludes: 'This is the true God and eternal life.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is 1 John 5:20.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 2 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us an understanding, that we may know Him who is true; and we are in Him who is true, in His Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of 1 John 5:20.

questions.question: What is the primary truth affirmed about Jesus Christ at the end of the memory verse (1 John 5:20)?
options: A. He is the Creator of all things
B. He is the Word made flesh
C. He is the true God and eternal life
D. He is the Redeemer of mankind
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly ends with 'This is the true God and eternal life.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline in the Week 2 lesson?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'HIS DIVINITY'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, which Old Testament reference is used to show that Jesus is a member of the Godhead?
questions.explanation: Genesis 1:26a is cited directly after stating Jesus is a member of the Godhead.

questions.question: According to Colossians 2:9, the Bible clearly states that the Godhead resides in Jesus Christ spiritually, but not bodily.
questions.explanation: The text says 'The Bible clearly states that the Godhead resides in Jesus Christ bodily (Colossians 2:9).'

questions.question: In Hebrews 1:8, who refers to Jesus as God?
questions.explanation: The lesson states: 'God the Father refers to Jesus as God (Hebrews 1:8).'

questions.question: Where is this prophetic announcement of Christ's deity as 'the mighty God' found in the Old Testament?
questions.explanation: Outline I cites Isaiah 9:6 for the prophecy of Christ as 'the mighty God'.

questions.question: Jesus says in John 10:30, 'I and My Father are ________'.
questions.explanation: The quote from John 10:30 is 'I and My Father are one'.

questions.question: Jesus announcing His identity as 'I AM' in John 8:58 is a direct application of the Old Testament name for God found where?
questions.explanation: The lesson points out that 'I AM' is a direct application of Exodus 3:14.

questions.question: According to Outline I, Jesus rebuked people for worshipping Him in Matthew 2:11 and Luke 24:52.
questions.explanation: The lesson explicitly states: 'He never rebukes people for worshipping Him.'

questions.question: In John 20:28, what declaration did Thomas the disciple make to Jesus?
options: A. 'You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.'
C. 'Teacher, we know you are from God.'
questions.explanation: Thomas declared 'My Lord and my God.'

questions.question: Which apostle describes Jesus as '...the great God and our Saviour, Jesus Christ' in Titus 2:13?
questions.explanation: The text states 'The apostle Paul describes Him as... (Titus 2:13)'.

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline in the Week 2 lesson?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'REASONS JESUS IS GOD'.

questions.question: According to Outline II, what provision only works if Jesus is God?
options: A. The provision of the Holy Spirit
C. The provision of daily bread
questions.explanation: The text states: 'The provision of salvation only works if Jesus is God...'

questions.question: Jesus is God incarnate, the eternal ________ and Omega.
questions.explanation: Citing Revelation 1:8, Jesus is called the 'eternal Alpha and Omega'.

questions.question: According to Acts 5:31 and Luke 5:20-24, Jesus has the right to forgive sins, which is something only God can do.
questions.explanation: The outline explicitly states that Jesus has the right to forgive sins, an action only God can perform.

questions.question: Where is the title 'judge the living and the dead' found in reference to Jesus in the lesson?
questions.verse_text: judge the living and the dead
questions.explanation: The lesson attributes this phrase to 2 Timothy 4:1.

questions.question: According to the lesson, what is the formal name of God in the Old Testament?
questions.explanation: The text refers to Jehovah as '(the formal name of God)'.

questions.question: Which title belonging uniquely to Jehovah in the Old Testament is used for Jesus in the New Testament (Titus 2:13-14; Rev 5:9)?
questions.explanation: The lesson specifically uses the example of 'Redeemer' (Psalms 19:14; 78:35).

questions.question: What does the name 'Emmanuel' mean according to Matthew 1:23?
questions.explanation: The lesson defines Emmanuel as 'God with us'.

questions.question: According to John 1:3 and Colossians 1:16-17, what specific action accomplished only by God is credited to Jesus?
options: A. He created and sustains the universe
B. He parted the Red Sea
C. He spoke from the burning bush
D. He brought the flood upon the earth
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions 'He created and sustains the universe'.

questions.question: Which divine attribute, representing God being present everywhere, is credited to Jesus in Matthew 18:20 and 28:20?
questions.explanation: The lesson lists Omnipresence and cites those verses.

questions.question: Which attribute representing all-knowing capability is assigned to Jesus with the reference Matthew 16:21?
questions.explanation: Matthew 16:21 is cited for Omniscience.

questions.question: Which attribute representing all-powerful capability is assigned to Jesus with the reference Luke 7:13-15?
questions.explanation: Luke 7:13-15 is cited for Omnipotence.

questions.question: What does the conclusion command Christians to do regarding the deity of Jesus?
options: A. Believe it silently and keep it to themselves
B. Debate it with people of other religions constantly
C. Believe in it, and boldly proclaim and defend it scripturally
D. Study it until they can understand all mysteries
questions.explanation: The conclusion says 'Christians should not only believe in the deity of Jesus but must be bold to proclaim and defend it scripturally.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'All things were made through Him, and without Him nothing was made that was made.'
questions.verse_text: All things were made through Him, and without Him nothing was made that was made.
questions.explanation: This is verse 3 of the main Bible Passage, John 1:1-5.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (John 1:5), the light shines in the darkness, and the darkness comprehended it.
questions.explanation: John 1:5 says 'and the darkness did not comprehend it.'

questions.question: And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us, and we beheld His glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father, full of ________ and truth.
questions.explanation: John 1:14 says 'full of grace and truth'.

questions.question: According to 2 Peter 1:1, how did Peter describe Jesus?
options: A. the great God and our Saviour
B. the Son of the living God
C. of God and our Saviour Jesus Christ
questions.explanation: The text notes that Peter says '...of God and our Saviour Jesus Christ'.

questions.question: The right to forgive sins, which Jesus exercised in Luke 5:20-24, was shared by the apostles.
questions.explanation: The outline states that forgiving sins 'is something only God can do'.

questions.question: What phrase did Jesus use in John 8:58 to directly apply the Old Testament name of God to Himself?
questions.explanation: The lesson quotes 'before Abraham was, I AM' as the direct application.

questions.question: According to the introduction and Matthew 16:13-16, what does the Bible tell us about Jesus in contrast to other religions?
options: A. He was merely a good teacher
B. He was the final prophet
C. He was infinitely more than a prophet, good teacher, or godly man combined
D. He was a symbol of divine nature
questions.explanation: The introduction states 'the Bible tells us that Jesus was infinitely more than the three combined'.
